what_it_does: This file is a ledger attachment record that associates an image resource with the design-source channel by storing metadata (id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and timestamp) but no body content.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no Markdown body.
It declares id 'att-db6f9c0b', channel 'design-source', and attachment_type 'images'.
An ISO 8601 timestamp '2026-06-22T11:31:01.943480+00:00' is recorded under 'added'.
No code symbols, imports, or executable content are present.
standouts: The target uses a channel-scoped 'image://' URI pointing to 'recent-pitches/p-08'.
The attachment_type is 'images', making this a typed media reference.
The id prefix 'att-' and filename match, suggesting a deterministic attachment identity.
conventions: Uses an 'att-' prefixed identifier and a matching filename.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
References the target through a URI scheme rather than a filesystem path.
concerns: The target URI cannot be resolved or validated from the file alone.
No uploader, author, or provenance field is present.
No MIME type, dimensions, or alt text are recorded for the image.
The relationship between this attachment and pitch 'p-08' is implicit in the URI only.
notes: A minimal, timestamped ledger entry that registers an image attachment for a recent design-source pitch via a URI reference.
questions: What is the actual image file or format at 'image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-08'?
Who added this attachment and on behalf of which message or pitch record?
Is 'p-08' a pitch identifier defined elsewhere, and how is the attachment ordered or versioned?
Does the 'image://' URI resolve to a local store, object storage, or generated asset?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does not state an explicit purpose, but its fields match the apparent purpose of a channel attachment ledger entry: it records that an image is attached to the design-source channel.
apparent_themes: Lightweight metadata records for design artifacts
Separation of binary assets from descriptive ledger entries
intention_signals: It appears intended to register and index an image asset associated with a recent design pitch, making the attachment discoverable by channel and target without storing the image inline.
novelty: It uses a channel-scoped 'image://' URI scheme to locate a pitch asset rather than a conventional file path or URL.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped link between a channel and an external image asset, supporting attachment indexing and retrieval while keeping binary content out of the ledger.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment ledger entry that records an image attachment for the design-source channel targeting 'image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-08' with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register and make discoverable an image asset linked to a recent design-source pitch.
